rulings.Case Title: JANNA CRANDALL VS. CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order & For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 19, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT J.B. PLUMBING COMPANY'S Motion For A Protective Order & For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied as moot. Motion to compel the deposition was granted on December 11, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 19, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As to the Special Interrogatories: Nos. 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 12, 14, 19, 21-31 inclusive, denied; Nos.13, 18 granted. Regarding overbroad interrogatories, see Obregon v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 424, 431. As to Requests for Documents: Nos. 39, 40, 41 denied; No. 36, granted in part, limited to the period beginning two years prior to her departure to the date of production, and the term DEFENDANT is limited to Bank of the West; No. 38, granted to the extent plaintiff agreed to produce documents within the specified category but did not, and has not confirmed by a verified writing that no such documents exist; No. 42, granted, except the request does NOT seek email sent by plaintiff, unless it is contained in a thread following an email that does fall within this category. With respect to Form Interrogatories ¿ Employment Law: Nos. 200.4, 207.2, 212.4(d) granted; Nos. 202.1, 202.2, 203.1, 210.4, 212.3, 212.5, 212.7 denied. Furthermore, plaintiff shall provide verifications for all previous responses to the discovery at issue in this motion within 15 days following entry of notice of this order. Plaintiff shall provide the further responses required by this order, without objection and verified, within 30 days following entry of notice of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CFO RICK, INC VS. KRAEMER, NICK, AKA DOORMAN PROPERTY MANAGEMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2013, Line 18: CROSS DEFENDANTS CFO RICK, INC.'S and RICK BELGARDE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not plead the element of breach. The FACC alleges that the contractual relationship was terminated on December 27, 2013 (FACC, 55-56), prior to the date performance was due. (FACC, 37.) The demurrer to the second cause of action for fraud is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The FACC alleges that Cross-Defendants made truthful but misleading statements. The FACC generally pleads that Cross-Defendants are accountants who mislead Cross-Complainant into believing they were Certified Public Accountants ("CPAs"). The FACC does not plead that Cross-Defendants being CPAs was material information to Cross-Defendants. (See Morgan v. AT&T Wireless Services, Inc. (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1235, 1262.) The demurrer to the third cause of action for extortion is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The FACC does not plead a threat to do unlawful injury to a person or property. (Cal. Penal Code § 519.) The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for intentional tort is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is duplicative of other causes of action already alleged in the FACC. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage is overruled. The FACC pleads that the intentional acts of Cross-Defendants interrupted its business operations. (See e.g. FACC 46-48.) The demurrer to the ninth cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The FACC does not adequately plead that the public would be misled by Cross-Defendants stating they are accountants or an accounting firm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MARLON ESTACIO PAGTAKHAN VS. THE SF EXAMINER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2013, Line 19: DEFENDANTS THE SF EXAMINER, TODD VOGT, MIKE ALDAX, and ARI BURACK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, as to the 3rd, 5th, 6th and 7th causes of action and overruled as to the 1st, 2nd and 4th causes of action. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege causes of action for general negligence, violation of the right to publicity, intentional infliction of emotion distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress. For the 3rd cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled the elements to allege general negligence, and there is no cognizable claim for failure to have defamatory articles retracted and/or deleted from internet medium. For the 5th cause of action for violation of right of publicity, Plaintiff requests leave to amend in his opposition to the demurrer, and leave to amend will be granted. For the 6th cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, Plaintiff has not pled that Defendant's conduct was outrageous. (See Judicial Council of California Civil Jury Instructions ("CACI") Jury Instruction number 1600(1).) For the 7th cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress, Plaintiff has not pled that Defendant's negligence was a substantial factor in causing Plaintiff' serious emotional distress. (See CACI Jury Instruction number 1620(3).) The 1st, 2nd and 4th causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER STERN VS. FACEBOOK, INC. et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2013, Line 24: DEFENDANT LULUVISE INC., LULUVISE LTD, and ALEXANDRA CHONG'S Motions To Strike Complaint Under CCP 425.16 And For Judgment On The Pleadings Granted, in part, as to the anti-SLAPP motion pertaining to Defendants Luluvise, Inc. and Luluvise Limited. Off calendar as moot as the motion for judgment on the pleadings as to Defendants Luluvise, Inc. and Luluvise Limited in light of the ruling granting their unopposed anti-SLAPP motion. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC. VS. NICO MARTIN PRESENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Applicaiton Andhearing For Right To Attach Order Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2013, Line 22: PLAINTIFF BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC.'S Applicaiton And Hearing For Right To Attach Order Of Writ Of Attachment Granted, in part, as to Defendant Nico Martin Presents, Inc. dba Nico Martin Presents, and denied as to Defendant Nico Martin aka Nicolas Martin, individually and dba Nico Martin Presents. Attachment in the amount of $ 4,992.49 shall issue against Defendant Nico Martin Presents, Inc. dba Nico Martin Presents. Before the issuance of the writ of attachment, Plaintiff BiRite Restaurant Supply, Inc. shall file an undertaking of $ 10,000.00. (CCP §§ 489.210, 489.220.) Plaintiff BiRite Retaurant Supply, Inc. applies for a writ of attachment against Defendants Nico Martin Presents, Inc. dba Nico Martin Presents (collectively "Nico Martin Presents") and Nico Martin aka Nicolas Martin, individually and dba Nico Martin Presents (collectively "Mr. Martin"). (Application, 2.) Plaintiff has established the probable validity of its claim against Nico Martin Presents and that the sum is a fixed and ascertainable amount of 4,992.49. (Newell Decl., 4, Exhibit A.) Plaintiff has not established the probable validity of its claim against Mr. Martin. (CCP § 484.090.) The open book account identifies the account holder as "Nico Martin Presents", and not Mr. Martin. (Newell Decl., Exhibit A.) Plaintiff has not presented sufficient evidence to demonstrate that Mr. Martin does business as Nico Martin Presents. In the Complaint, Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Martin signed a personal guaranty. (Complaint, 14, Exhibit B.) However, the Complaint is not verified as averred in the Application. (Compare Complaint; with Application, 7a, Cook Decl., p. 1:10-11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required provide proof of filing an undertaking in the amount of $ 10,000.00 and to prepare a proposed order on the Judicial Council form with the name and address of the Plaintiff's counsel. =302/MJM
